body:
## Summary

Today, when a user says **"Report issue"** inside an agentic session driven by Formal AI, we file a short natural-language issue (example: #821). That is not enough. The maintainer who picks it up gets the words the user typed but **none of the surrounding context** — the full conversation, every tool call and its output, tokens, timings, provider/model, working directory, and the Formal AI server-side logs for that turn.

We want **automatic, complete, agentic error reporting** across *every* surface Formal AI is used from — the HTTP API **and** every agentic harness (opencode, Claude Code, Codex, agent CLI/TUI, …) — where the entire context is captured and uploaded, and **everything is uploaded in [Links Notation](../data/seed), not JSON**.

This is the umbrella issue collecting all requirements so the implementing agent can focus on one clearly-scoped deliverable. Prior art: the **Report issue** scaffolding (#78) and [`docs/upload-memory.md`](../docs/upload-memory.md) already establish the pattern of attaching a full `formal_ai_bundle` (`.lino`) to a bug report from the web demo. This issue extends that pattern from the browser to **API + agentic CLI/TUI harnesses**, and makes it automatic rather than a manual export.

---

## Motivation

Concrete case that triggered this: an **opencode** session (`ses_078007767ffecjImFV0GYvoKQu`) using the `formalai` provider / `formal-ai` model. opencode (≥ ~1.18) stores conversations in a single SQLite DB (`~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db`), **not** per-session JSON files. When such a session reports an issue, we currently lose:

- the full message list and every tool call (`websearch`, `webfetch`, …) with inputs **and** outputs,
- per-step tokens / cost / snapshot ids,
- provider/model, agent, session slug, working directory, opencode version,
- and — critically — the **Formal AI server-side logs** for the same conversation.

A maintainer cannot reproduce from `> Search for Elon Musk` + `> Report issue` alone.

---

## Requirements

### R0 — Ask the user WHAT to report before collecting anything
"Report" / "Report issue" is ambiguous, and the user may mean different things by it. **Before** Formal AI collects or uploads any data, it MUST ask the user what they want, and only then act. At minimum the choices are:

- **Full agentic-harness conversation log** — the client-side transcript from opencode/Claude/Codex/… (messages + tool calls + metadata).
- **Full server-side conversation log** — Formal AI's own server logs for the conversation (see R2/R5).
- **The issue itself, trimmed to fit** — a GitHub issue capped at GitHub's issue size limit, carrying the last messages that fit (with a link/attachment to the full context for the rest).
- **Report to Formal AI itself** — the user may not mean "open a GitHub issue" at all; "report" may mean reporting the problem *to Formal AI* (teach it, log it, file internally). Do not assume GitHub.

If the user chooses to file the issue, ask a **second** question: **what to include** in it (harness log? server log? both? trimmed vs. linked full context?).

**Interaction mechanism:** use the harness's most advanced supported UI. If the harness supports structured questions with selectable options (opencode/Claude/Codex all do), present the choices as options. If it does not, fall back to asking a plain-text question and parsing plain-text answers (accept the obvious phrasings for each option). Detect the capability and prefer the richest available.

This gating question comes first; R1–R7 describe what happens **after** the user has chosen.

### R1 — Automatic, full-context reporting in agentic mode (API + harnesses)
On **"Report issue"** (or an equivalent trigger), the report MUST include the **entire conversation context**, not just the last user/assistant messages. This must work whether the request comes through:
- the **HTTP API** (`/api/openai/v1`, `/api/anthropic/v1`, `/api/gemini/v1beta`, `/api/vertex/v1`), where the conversation context is detectable from the request payload, and
- an **agentic harness** (opencode, Claude Code, Codex, agent CLI/TUI, …), where context is detectable either from tool calls Formal AI itself makes/receives, or by the harness invoking a Formal AI bash command / API route.

Detection MUST be automatic — Formal AI should recognize "this is an agentic-coding conversation and I have (or can fetch) its full context" without the user hand-assembling anything.

### R2 — Upload server logs for the conversation
Beyond the client-side transcript, the report MUST attach the **Formal AI server logs** that correspond to the conversation (matched by session/conversation id and turn), with all details. See R5 (verbose-by-default) so these logs are actually complete.

### R3 — A `/api` route to expose conversation context to tools
Add an HTTP route (e.g. under `/api/...`) that **any agentic tool using Formal AI bash commands can call** to fetch the actual conversation data (transcript + server logs + metadata) for a given conversation/session id. This is the machine-readable source of truth agentic harnesses pull from when assembling a report. Output of this route MUST be **Links Notation** (see R4).

### R4 — Everything uploaded as Links Notation, not JSON (by default)
When uploading conversation details from **any** tool (opencode, Claude, Codex, agent CLI, …), the payload MUST be converted to **Links Notation** by default, not raw JSON. This is consistent with Formal AI's existing use of Links Notation as the reviewable text format for seed data, packages, and traces. JSON MAY remain available behind an explicit opt-in flag, but the default (and what lands in the issue) is `.lino`.

**Use the native Links Notation SEQUENCE idiom for ordered collections.** Do not emit synthetic index keys (`message_0 … message_N`, `part_0 …`). An ordered list is the singular header key repeated once per element — `messages` followed by repeated `message` records, `parts` followed by repeated `part` records — mirroring the repeated-`expression` shape in `experiments/issue_715_nested_newline_probe.rs`. Prefer the multiline form and **use as few parentheses as possible**: parentheses are only for inline *scalar* lists (`outputs ("a" "b" "c")`). Verified example (round-trips through the `links-notation` grammar):

```
conversation ses_XXXX
  messages
    message
      role user
      parts
        part
          data
            text "Find hive-mind-control center folder on my desktop"
            type text
    message
      role assistant
      parts
        part
          data
            type tool
            tool websearch
            state
              status completed
              input
                query "hive mind control center folder on my desktop"
```

**Value encoding rules that MUST hold (verified against `links-notation` 0.13):**
- `:` is structurally significant — a bare `https://…` or `a:b` **desyncs the parser**. Any value that is not a plain bare token (`[A-Za-z0-9_.\-/]+` with no `:`) MUST be quoted.
- Delimiter selection like `lino-objects-codec`: use `"…"`, or `'…'` when the value contains `"`, and fall back to base64 (tagged `b64:`) when a value contains **both** quote characters. Base64 is pure `[A-Za-z0-9+/=]`, so it cannot desync the grammar — the doubled-quote escape (`""`) was observed to desync inside long, newline-escaped tool outputs when accumulated across a whole document.
- Values are flattened onto one physical line (CR/LF/TAB escaped), because the notation is line-oriented.

### R5 — `--verbose` on by default, across the whole of Formal AI
Turn `--verbose` mode **on by default** for the entire `formal-ai` (server + CLI + harness integrations), so every detail is printed to all logs. This guarantees R2's server-log attachment is actually complete rather than a sparse summary. (A quiet/`--silent` flag MAY exist to opt back out.)

### R6 — Formal AI converts arbitrary JSON → Links Notation via agentic tools
Formal AI itself must be smart enough to convert **any** JSON to Links Notation, using the tools available in any agentic coding environment (i.e. the same `lino-objects-codec` / codec path already used for seed/bundle export, driven from an agentic tool call). The opencode-specific extractor below (R7) is the reference implementation of that conversion for one source; the general capability should not be limited to opencode.

### R7 — Reference extractor (already built & tested)
A working reference script that reads an opencode session from `opencode.db` (read-only, stdlib-only, no deps) and emits the full conversation + metadata as Links Notation:

**Gist:** https://gist.github.com/konard/158786543dbfb26efa8f3437d41d20dd

```bash
opencode-conversation-to-lino.py <session_id> [--db PATH] [-o OUT.lino]
```

Tested on `ses_078007767ffecjImFV0GYvoKQu` → 606 lines of Links Notation capturing every message, tool call (input + output), tokens, snapshot ids, provider/model, and working directory. The output uses the **native sequence idiom from R4** (repeated `message` / `part`, no index keys) and was **verified to round-trip through the `links-notation` 0.13 grammar** (parses cleanly; all 9 `message` records and all `part` records preserved; `websearch`/`webfetch` tool calls preserved). Sample of the output shape:

```
conversation ses_078007767ffecjImFV0GYvoKQu
  source
    tool opencode
    storage sqlite
    notation links-notation
  session
    directory /Users/konard/Code/evirma/wb-shelf
    model
      id formal-ai
      providerID formalai
    tokens_input 338956
    version 1.18.4
  messages
    message
      role user
      parts
        part
          data
            text "Find hive-mind-control center folder on my desktop"
            type text
    message
      role assistant
      parts
        part
          data
            type tool
            tool websearch
            state
              status completed
              input
                query "hive mind control center folder on my desktop"
```

It implements the R4 value-encoding rules (colon-safe quoting, delimiter selection, base64 fallback for both-quote values — only 2 of the large tool outputs fell back to base64, and they decode losslessly). This script should become the opencode adapter behind R3's `/api` route and R6's general JSON→Links-Notation conversion.

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Before any data is collected, Formal AI **asks the user what to report** (harness log / server log / trimmed issue / report-to-Formal-AI-itself), then, if filing an issue, asks **what to include** — using the harness's structured-options UI when available and plain text otherwise (R0).
- [ ] Reporting an issue from an agentic session (API **or** harness) attaches the **full conversation** as Links Notation, not a bare NL summary (contrast with #821).
- [ ] Ordered collections use the **repeated-key sequence** idiom (no `message_0…N` / `part_0…N`), and the emitted `.lino` round-trips through the `links-notation` grammar (R4).
- [ ] Formal AI **server logs** for that conversation are attached, and are complete (R5).
- [ ] A `/api` route returns the conversation context (transcript + logs + metadata) in Links Notation for a given conversation/session id, callable from agentic bash commands.
- [ ] `--verbose` is the default for server + CLI + integrations; an explicit opt-out exists.
- [ ] Uploads default to `.lino` (Links Notation); JSON only behind an explicit flag.
- [ ] A general JSON→Links-Notation conversion path exists (not opencode-only), with the opencode extractor (R7) wired in as one adapter.
- [ ] opencode adapter reproduces the tested output on `ses_078007767ffecjImFV0GYvoKQu`.

---

## Notes for the implementing agent

- Build on the existing `formal_ai_bundle` / `.lino` export path (#78, `docs/upload-memory.md`, `lino-objects-codec`) rather than inventing a second serialization.
- opencode stores conversations in **SQLite** (`opencode.db`: `session` / `message` / `part` tables, JSON in `data` columns), not JSON files — open it **read-only** so a running opencode is never disturbed.
- Keep the reporting flow deterministic and reviewable: sorted keys, stable ordering by `(time_created, id)`.
- The `/api` route and the harness bash-command path should return the **same** Links Notation for the same conversation id (single source of truth).

---

*Filed to consolidate all requirements for full-context agentic error reporting into a single actionable issue.*

comments.body:
Updated the requirements with **R0 — ask the user what to report before collecting anything** (full harness log / full server log / trimmed GitHub issue / report-to-Formal-AI-itself; then a follow-up on what to include), using the harness's structured-options UI when supported and plain-text Q&A otherwise.

Also corrected the Links Notation guidance (R4/R7): ordered collections now use the native **repeated-key sequence** idiom (`messages` → repeated `message`, `parts` → repeated `part`) with no `message_0…N` index keys and as few parentheses as possible. The reference extractor was updated accordingly and **verified to round-trip through the `links-notation` 0.13 grammar**, including the colon-quoting / delimiter-selection / base64-fallback value rules. Gist updated: https://gist.github.com/konard/158786543dbfb26efa8f3437d41d20dd

title: Full-context agentic error reporting: upload entire conversation + server logs as Links Notation (API + opencode/claude/codex harnesses)
